task_completion.description: Select the 'Thesis Chapter 3' collection in Zotero
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Zotero application is open and the 'Thesis Chapter 3' collection is highlighted or selected in the left-hand pane.

task_completion.description: Open the 'Create Bibliography from Collection' dialog
task_completion.visual_evidence: A context menu showing 'Create Bibliography from Collection...' being clicked, or the 'Create Bibliography from Collection' dialog box is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Choose 'American Psychological Association 7th edition' as the citation style
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the bibliography dialog box, 'American Psychological Association 7th edition' is highlighted or selected in the list of available citation styles.

task_completion.description: Choose to save the bibliography as an HTML file
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the bibliography dialog box, the 'Save as HTML' radio button or output method option is selected.

task_completion.description: Save the generated bibliography to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or save dialog showing the directory '/home/ga/Documents/' and the filename 'thesis_chapter3_references.html' being saved.

task_completion.description: The exported HTML file contains the expected APA-formatted citations
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'thesis_chapter3_references.html' is seen in a file explorer, browser, or text editor, showing 5 formatted references including authors like Shannon, Turing, Vaswani, LeCun, and He.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Zotero GUI to export the bibliography
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Zotero application open and the agent clicking through its menus and dialogs, rather than using a terminal script to generate the file.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's save dialog to create the file
integrity.visual_evidence: The file creation process is shown via the Zotero 'Save' dialog, not by creating a dummy file using a text editor or the 'touch' command in the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used the built-in export functionality rather than manually typing out the references
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using the 'Create Bibliography' feature, not typing authors and titles into an HTML file manually.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target collection is 'Thesis Chapter 3'. The citation style must be 'American Psychological Association 7th edition'. The output must be an HTML file saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/thesis_chapter3_references.html'. The exported bibliography should contain exactly 5 entries corresponding to seminal papers by Claude E. Shannon (1948), Alan M. Turing (1950), Ashish Vaswani (2017), Yann LeCun (2015), and He et al. ('Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition', 2016).
